rulings.Case Title: YUE YING WANG VS. NATURAL WONDER A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Special Interoggs-Set One
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 12, 2012, Line 1, DEFENDANT NATURAL WONDER A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY, ZHONG FU, ZHI LIU Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Special Interoggs-Set One Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: THE MOTION OF DEFENDANT AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT NATURAL WONDER COSMETICS, LL.C ("NATURAL WONDER")' MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF YUE YING WANG TO PROVIDE FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NO.8,9,10,13,26,27,29,30,31,32,33,34&35 IS GRANTED. THESE INTERROGATORIES CLEARLY CALL FOR INFORMATION THAT IS RELEVANT TO THE ISSUES IN THE LITIGATION AND THE COURT DOES NOT BELIEVE THAT ANY OF THE OBJECTIONS TO THEM JUSTIFY A REFUSAL TO ANSWER IN FULL. PLAINTIFF WILL FILE, WITHOUT OBJECTIONS, RESPONSES WITHIN 15 DAYS OF THIS ORDER. COUNSEL FOR THE MOVING PARTY SHOULD PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT TO COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER TO THE COURT OF SIGNATURE AT THE HEARING ON SEPTEMBER 12TH. NO SANCTIONS WILL BE IMPOSED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: YUE YING WANG VS. NATURAL WONDER A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Special Interoggs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 12, 2012, Line 1, DEFENDANT NATURAL WONDER A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY, ZHONG FU, ZHI LIU Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Special Interoggs Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: CROSS-COMPLAINT NATURAL WONDER COSMETIC, LLC ("NATURAL WONDER")'S MOTION TO COMPEL CROSS-DEFENDANT MAN YU GUAN ("GUAN") TO MAKE FURTHER REPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES 14,15,18,21,22,23,24,25&26 IS GRANTED. IT APPEARS FROM THE PAPERS THAT GUAN HAS STIPULATED THAT IT WILL MAKE FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES 14,15,23&24, BUT CONTINUES TO REFUSE TO SUPPLEMENT THE RESPONSE TO THE OTHER INTERROGATORIES THAT ARE THE SUBJECT OF THIS MOTION. THE REMAINING INTERROGATORIES ARE NOT OBJECTIONABLE, CALL FOR RELEVANT INFORMATION AND SHOULD BE ANSWERED IN FULL WITHOUT ANY FURTHER OBJECTION. RESPONSES WILL BE SERVED NO LATER THAN 15 DAY FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. COUNSEL FOR THE MOVING PARTY SHOULD PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT TO GUAN'S COUNSEL FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER IT TO THE COURT FOR SIGNATURE AT THE TIME OF THE SCHEDULED HEARING. NO SANCTIONS WILL BE IMPOSED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: URI BEN HUR et al VS. YES TO, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, September 12, 2012, Line 6, PLAINTIFFS URI HUR, THE SEVENTH MILLENNIUM, LTD.'S MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS. Denied. However, defendants' motion for summary adjudication is continued to October 25, 2012 with the opposition and reply papers to be filed in accordance with CCP 437c. A stay of the action is unwarranted, although a short continuance of defendants' motion will enable the individual plaintiff to file substantive opposition papers which may, as a practical matter, apply fully to the corporate plaintiff. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: EVGENI LITVAK et al VS. YURI GALASHOV et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross Complainant Svetlana Samarina'S Motion To Quash Defendant Yuri Galashov'S Deposition Subpoenas For Production Of Business Records From Citibank And Request For Monetary Sanction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 12, 2012, Line 6, Cross Complainant Svetlana Samarina'S Motion To Quash Defendant Yuri Galashov'S Deposition Subpoenas For Production Of Business Records From Citibank And Request For Monetary Sanction Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: CROSS-COMPLAINT SVETLANA SAMARINA'S MOTION TO QUASH DEFENDANT YURI GALASHOV'S DEPOSITION NOTICE TO CITIBANK IS GRANTED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO DEFENDANT' RIGHT, IF HE CHOOSES, TO REISSUE A DEPOSITION SUBPOENA TO CITIBANK LIMITED TO THE PRODUCTION OF ACCOUNT STATEMENTS OF ANY ACCOUNT HELD IN THE NAME OF SVETLANA SAMARINA AND COPIES OF ANY CHECKS DEPOSITED INTO ANY SUCH ACCOUNT DURING THE PERIOD FROM APRIL 1, 2008 TO THE PRESENT. NO SANCTIONS WILL BE IMPOSED. COUNSEL FOR THE MOVING PARTY WILL PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT TO COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER TO THE COURT AT THE HEARING ON SEPT. 12TH. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DEFENDANT ROBERTO BARRERA RAMOS' MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF TO RESPOND TO HIS REQUEST FOR STATEMENT SETTING FORTH THE NATURE AND AMOUNT OF DAMAGES IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF WILL FILE HER RESPONSE WITHIN 15 DAYS OF THE DATE OF THIS ORDER WITHOUT OBJECTION EXCEPT TO AN ASSERTION, IF APPROPRIATE, OF AN ATTORNEY/CLIENT PRIVILEGE. COUNSEL FOR THE MOVING PARTY WITH PREPARED AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT TO COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNATURE AT THE HEARING ON SEPT. 12TH. NO ADDITIONAL SANCTIONS WILL BE IMPOSED BEYOND THAT SET FORTH ABOVE. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: MXB HOLDINGS, LP A DELAWARE LIMITED PARTNERSHIP et al VS. ADAM LEVIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel The Production Of Docs By Deft Criterion
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, September 12, 2012, Line 16, PLAINTIFF MXB HOLDINGS, LP, MICHAEL JACKSON, HECKLER CONSULTANCY, SV ANGEL, LLC'S Motion To Compel The Production Of Documents By Defendant Criterion. Granted. Regardless of the e-service issue, plaintiffs have shown that the requests for production were served by hand. Defendants must provide complete responses without objections no later than September 28. Monetary sanctions are awarded to plaintiffs in the amount of $3685 (1 hour at $400 per hour, 10 hours at $325 per hour and $60 in costs) to be paid no later than November 2. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SEIFER, MURKEN, DESPINA, JAMES & TEICHMAN VS. BAR ASSOCIATION OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Bar Association Of San Francisco'S Demurrer To Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, September 12, 2012, Line 17, Defendant Bar Association Of San Francisco's Demurrer To Complaint. Overruled. The claims are adequately pled. From the face of the Complaint, it is not clear there was arbitration conducted pursuant to Bus. and Prof. Code §§6200-6206 therefore it is not clear Defendant has immunity under Bus. and Prof. Code §6200(f). The Court will not consider the common law arbitral immunity argument raised only in Defendant's reply papers as Plaintiff did not have an opportunity to address that argument. The Court declines to take judicial notice of the requested documents. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
